objects.abstract: Geopolitical analysis (EN) / Middle East Kinetic and Cyber Conflict
objects.content:
Sector: Energy, Critical Infrastructure, Government

Theme: Middle East Kinetic and Cyber Conflict

Massive military strikes (Operations Epic Fury/Roaring Lion) against Iran followed by widespread cyber-attacks. Iran's internet connectivity dropped to 1-4%. Iranian proxies are retaliating with DDoS and camera hacking against Israel and Western allies.

Tags: Iran, Israel, USA, Kinetic Cyber Integration

objects.name: Coruna: Advanced iOS Exploit Kit
objects.description:
A powerful exploit kit featuring 5 full exploit chains and 23 exploits targeting iOS versions 13.0 to 17.2.1. It has circulated among surveillance vendors, espionage groups (UNC6353), and financially motivated actors (UNC6691).

Analysis:
The Coruna kit demonstrates the industrialization of zero-day exploits. It uses sophisticated JavaScript obfuscation and fingerprinting to deliver specific payloads based on the iPhone model. It targets old vulnerabilities like CVE-2024-23222 and CVE-2023-43000.

Recommendations:
- Update iPhones to the latest iOS version immediately.
- Enable Lockdown Mode for high-risk users.

objects.name: EvilProxy
objects.description: Tool: EvilProxy

objects.name: AitM frameworks
objects.description: Tool: AitM frameworks

objects.name: OAuth Redirection Abuse in Phishing Campaigns
objects.description:
Attackers leverage legitimate OAuth redirection flows to bypass email security. By triggering errors with invalid scopes, they force the identity provider to redirect victims to malicious infrastructure.

Analysis:
This is an identity-based threat that abuses standards-compliant behavior. It is effective even against users with MFA when AitM frameworks like EvilProxy are used to steal session cookies.

Recommendations:
- Restrict user consent for third-party OAuth applications.
- Monitor for unusual redirect URIs in OAuth application registrations.

objects.content: The cyber threat landscape on March 4, 2026, is dominated by a massive escalation in the Middle East, where kinetic operations (Operations Epic Fury and Roaring Lion) are tightly integrated with cyber-espionage and infrastructure sabotage. Simultaneously, the discovery of the 'Coruna' exploit kit reveals a sophisticated secondary market for iOS zero-days, transitioning from surveillance vendors to state actors and cybercriminals. Ransomware remains a critical industrial threat, with groups like Qilin and Anubis expanding their victimology, while identity-based threats abusing OAuth protocols and AI-targeted prompt injections represent the next frontier of defensive challenges.
